title: Oxydectes cubana
taxonomy: Oxydectes cubana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Oxydectes cubana is a member of the genus Oxydectes, which is a group of plants that are native to the Caribbean region. The plant is also known to be closely related to other species within the Commelinaceae family, such as those in the genera Tradescantia and Gonatopus. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Oxydectes cubana within the plant kingdom.
